description: Define the public Arc and Result types and the ErrInfeasible sentinel in mcf.go.
commit: feat: add public Arc and Result types for min-cost flow API
steps: Create mcf.go with `package mcf` importing `errors` and `github.com/holiman/uint256`.
Define `type Arc struct { Source, Target int; Cost int64; Capacity, Flow *uint256.Int }`. Callers set Source/Target/Cost/Capacity; Flow is written by Solve.
Define `type Result struct { TotalFlow *uint256.Int; TotalCost int64; Feasible bool }`.
Define `var ErrInfeasible = errors.New("mcf: problem is infeasible")`.
Do NOT define `Solve` here; its body lives in the wave-7 task.

Create mcf_test.go with `TestPublicTypesRoundTrip`: build an Arc whose Capacity = `new(uint256.Int).Lsh(uint256.NewInt(1), 200)` (> MaxUint64) with Flow = uint256.NewInt(0); assert Capacity.Cmp(new(uint256.Int).SetUint64(math.MaxUint64)) > 0. Build a Result{TotalFlow: uint256.NewInt(42), TotalCost: 100, Feasible: true} and assert all fields. Assert `errors.Is(ErrInfeasible, ErrInfeasible)`.
acceptance_criteria: mcf.go defines `Arc`, `Result`, `ErrInfeasible` with exactly the fields/values listed.
